category_title: Successful students with high online continuity and regular engagement
minimal_context: The student is posting about their favorite type of music and responding to peers on an online discussion board to practice using the target language.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please post a few lines describing your favorite type of music and then respond to two or three of your peers' postings.

STUDENT: Me gusta la música pop. Mi cantante favorito es Taylor Swift.

TUTOR: Okay, now respond to your peers.

STUDENT: A mí también me gusta la música pop.

TUTOR: Can you add anything else to your response?

STUDENT: No, I just wanted to indicate that we share the same preferences.

options.A: Warn the student that if they do not complete the discussion board assignment, they will risk losing access to their favorite online activities.
options.B: Firmly tell the student that this brief posting behavior is not allowed to continue on the discussion board once it has been addressed.
options.C: Prompt the student to propose ideas by asking for specific examples, such as asking them to share what they would do and why.
options.D: Instruct the student to use the journalist method, writing in short sentences like newspaper headlines to keep their discussion board posts brief and concise.
